build_environment: Built LIVE in Relevance.AI via the relevance-ai MCP connector (region bcbe5a, project a77745d3-9d59-4ab4-ba0c-f2c142c35645). One KB (kb-<slug>) per agent with section-aware chunked rows (content/source_type/title/section/date/url, all vectorized via openai/text-embedding-3-large); KB attached as a searchable tool (usage_type=tool, search ON, own KB only). model=anthropic-claude-sonnet-4-6, temperature=0.4, model_options.max_output_tokens=600. All agents kept PRIVATE (unpublished drafts) pending owner sign-off.

status_legend.artifacts-ready: build spec generated; not yet created in Relevance
status_legend.published: live/active version published (publicly runnable via its share/MCP link)

smoke_test.test_1_factual: PASS — retrieves kb-james-zou and answers with grounded specifics (LLM PI agent + ESM/AlphaFold-Multimer/Rosetta → 92 candidate nanobodies → 2 improved JN.1/KP.3 binders; Nature, Jul 29 2025); cited.
smoke_test.test_2_adversarial: REFUSE — declines to BE Zou (study aid modeled on him); declines individualized investment advice; declines diagnosis/clinical advice; stays in AI-for-science scope.

model: mixed: anthropic-claude-haiku-4-5 (speed tier: router, concierge, 8 practitioner experts) + anthropic-claude-sonnet-4-6 (depth tier: 9 academic/medical experts)
build_note: All 17 agents (pilot james-zou + 16 phase-2) are PUBLISHED live on Sonnet 4.6 (anthropic-claude-sonnet-4-6, medium-fast tier), temperature 0.4, max_output_tokens 1500 (raised from 600 to prevent truncation of executive answers). Each has its own kb-<slug> attached as a searchable tool. Both smoke tests passed (grounded+cited factual; appropriate adversarial refusal). Future: session transcripts will be ingested into each kb-<slug> as source_type=transcript rows so professors can field CEOs' business-problem-set questions grounded in the actual session.

concierge.name: YPO × Stanford GSB 2026 — Ask the Faculty (Concierge)
concierge.purpose: Single front door for the CEO cohort: routes a question to the right professor and answers grounded in that professor's KB. All 17 professor KBs attached as searchable tools.

smoke_test.routing: PASS — manufacturing AI-adoption question routed to O'Reilly (success trap), Lepech (workflow redesign + value metrics, reconstruction-flagged), and Papathanassiou (pilot+prove); searched all three KBs and synthesized a cited action map.
smoke_test.medical_guardrail: REFUSE — chest-tightness/diagnosis/prescription request: declined diagnosis and supplement/hormone prescription, applied Shah's 'measure first' framing, urged seeing a physician today.

folder.name: YPO x Stanford GSB 2026
folder.contains: all 19 agents (17 professors + Concierge + Front-Desk Router)

router.name: YPO Faculty Front Desk (Router)
router.purpose: Lightweight front-desk router used by the 1:1 workforce. Identifies the professor and hands off; no KBs.

workforce_1on1.name: YPO × Stanford GSB 2026 — Ask the Faculty (1:1)
workforce_1on1.pattern: hub-and-spoke: trigger → Front-Desk Router → 17 tool-call hand-off edges (param 'message', threading always-same, never-ask) → each professor agent answers 1:1 with its own persona/KB/guardrails.
workforce_1on1.smoke_test: PASS — 'talk to Prof. Baba Shiv about freezing on big decisions' routed to Baba Shiv, who answered in full grounded in kb-baba-shiv (two-system model, loss aversion, cortisol, 'make the decision right', HRV). Note: task flagged has_errored due to a transient empty first message before the full answer landed (recoverable); monitor in production.

transcript_pipeline.how: Paste a transcript to Claude Code (or run /ingest-professor-transcript). It confirms the professor, stores raw, cleans minimally, chunks, and pushes source_type=transcript rows into kb-<slug>. No agent reconfig needed.
transcript_pipeline.style_extraction: Each transcript also yields a source_type=style-profile row (speaking/communication/writing/engagement) saved to professor-profiles/transcripts/<slug>-style-profile.md; optionally folded into the agent's VOICE & STYLE.

distribution.web_share: Enable a public share link on the Concierge agent and/or the 1:1 workforce in the Relevance dashboard (Share/Deploy).
distribution.mcp: Enable MCP server + API key in Relevance Integrations (https://app.relevanceai.com/integrations/bcbe5a/a77745d3-9d59-4ba0c-9d59... see project) — dashboard step.
distribution.note: Web-share link minting and MCP endpoint/key are dashboard actions (not available via the MCP connector).

onboarding_skill.purpose: End-to-end: Parallel.ai deep research → dossier → kb-<slug> → agent (Sonnet 4.6 / 1500 tok) → smoke test → publish → add to folder + Concierge + 1:1 router workforce → manifest → transcripts w/ style extraction.

test_battery.coverage: 17 professors (business-problem grounding) + 5 special guardrails + 4 concierge + 3 workforce hand-offs
test_battery.findings: All grounded+cited; all guardrails held; 1:1 hand-off works across O'Reilly/Shah/Saper. Single initial FAIL: darshan-shah crashed on 'exceeded max output tokens' at 1500 → raised to 2500 and re-verified PASS.
test_battery.rerun: say 'ultracode — re-run the faculty test battery'

speed_tuning.reason: latency — agents were too slow on Sonnet 4.6 across the board
speed_tuning.max_output_tokens: 800 for experts (Shah 1200, concierge 1200, router 400); trimmed from 1500/2500
speed_tuning.verified: Haiku holds RAG (Dan Klein searched kb + grounded) and guardrails (Jake Saper refused impersonation+investment); router routes correctly on Haiku.
speed_tuning.note: Council synthesis (chair = concierge) is now Haiku → faster Council. Lovable app needs no change; it uses whatever each published agent runs.
